meta_title: Impact Doors in Boca Del Mar, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Boca Del Mar, FL. ESW & CWI brands, in-house crews, financing from $87/mo. Free quote today!
h1: Impact Doors for Boca Del Mar Homes: Strength, Style, and Storm-Ready Security
intro:
Boca Del Mar is one of Palm Beach County's most desirable residential communities — a polished, well-established enclave where tree-lined streets connect neighborhoods like Whisper Walk and Boca Lago to top-tier schools, recreational hubs like Patch Reef Park, and the broader amenities of Boca Raton. With average home values hovering around $470,000, protecting your investment here isn't just smart — it's essential. And one of the most effective ways Boca Del Mar homeowners are doing exactly that is by upgrading to professionally installed impact doors from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing.

While Boca Del Mar sits approximately six miles from the Atlantic coastline, don't let that distance inspire a false sense of security. Palm Beach County falls within a 150 mph wind zone, and the region has been tested repeatedly by major hurricane systems that caused widespread damage across inland communities just like this one. Whether it's an entry door, a sweeping set of sliding glass doors opening to your back patio, or elegant French doors separating your living spaces, every door in your home represents a potential vulnerability during a storm — and a significant energy and security liability year-round. Impact-rated doors eliminate those vulnerabilities in one upgrade.

Approximately 40% of homes in Boca Del Mar were built before 1994 — prior to the sweeping reforms that reshaped Florida's building codes in the wake of Hurricane Andrew. That means a substantial portion of homeowners in neighborhoods throughout the 33433 ZIP code are still relying on doors that were never designed to handle modern hurricane standards. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing specializes in helping these homeowners transition to impact door systems that meet or exceed today's Florida Building Code requirements, all while enhancing the curb appeal and daily functionality of their homes. From Boca Lago to Whisper Walk, we've helped hundreds of South Florida families make this critical upgrade — and we're ready to do the same for you.

section_why.h2: Why Boca Del Mar Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Impact Door Installation
section_why.body:
When you're investing in impact doors for a home worth nearly half a million dollars, the quality of your installation matters just as much as the quality of your products. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings more than 60 years of combined experience to every project we take on in Palm Beach County, and unlike many contractors who subcontract the actual installation work, we use 100% in-house crews from start to finish. That means the same experienced team that reviews your project plan is the team showing up at your Boca Del Mar home on installation day — no middlemen, no communication gaps, no surprises.

Our track record in South Florida speaks for itself. With 500+ completed homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, we understand the specific demands that the local climate, soil conditions, and architectural styles place on impact door installations. Boca Del Mar's mix of single-family homes, villas, and townhome communities means we regularly work with a wide range of door configurations — from wide-opening sliding glass systems in open-concept floor plans to solid impact entry doors on Mediterranean-style homes with detailed framing. Our team knows how to handle each scenario with precision.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing holds an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau and is fully licensed and insured throughout Florida. We pull all required permits through the Palm Beach County Building Division and manage the inspection process on your behalf, so you never have to navigate the bureaucracy alone. From your initial free consultation to the final inspection sign-off, we treat your project — and your home — with the respect it deserves. That commitment to service is exactly why we've earned the reputation as 'The Kings of Service' across South Florida.

Choosing Premier Impact Windows & Roofing also means gaining access to two of the industry's most trusted impact door product lines: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. These manufacturers produce doors purpose-built for Florida's demanding wind and weather environment, and our installation teams are factory-trained in their proper fitting and finishing. When you choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for your Boca Del Mar home, you're not just buying a door — you're buying decades of expertise installed correctly the first time.

section_history.h2: Storm History in Boca Del Mar: What the Record Says About the Risk
section_history.body:
Boca Del Mar and the surrounding communities of Palm Beach County have faced significant storm threats since 1990 — at least seven named storms have impacted the region, and three in particular left lasting impressions on local homeowners. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 was a watershed moment for South Florida. Though it made landfall in Miami-Dade County, its destructive force extended well northward, exposing the catastrophic vulnerability of homes built to pre-storm codes. It was Andrew that ultimately forced Florida to completely overhaul its building standards — but many Boca Del Mar homes that existed before that reform never received corresponding upgrades to their doors and windows.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 was perhaps the most directly impactful storm for Palm Beach County residents in recent memory. Wilma crossed the peninsula as a Category 3 system and caused extensive wind damage throughout Boca Raton and its neighboring communities, including Boca Del Mar. Homeowners who had not invested in impact-rated products faced devastating losses — shattered sliding glass doors, compromised entry doors, and the ensuing interior water damage that follows. Hurricane Irma in 2017 reinforced those lessons again, as sustained tropical-force winds battered communities across the county for hours.

The takeaway from this storm history is straightforward: Boca Del Mar is not shielded from hurricane risk by its inland position. Wind-borne debris and sustained gale-force pressure can destroy standard doors regardless of how many miles separate a home from the coast. Impact doors are not a luxury upgrade in this region — they are a practical, code-informed response to a documented pattern of storm damage that has affected real families in this exact community.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Palm Beach County
section_code.body:
Boca Del Mar falls within Palm Beach County's jurisdiction and is subject to the Florida Building Code (FBC), which mandates hurricane-protection standards for all exterior openings — including every door in your home. While the 33433 ZIP code is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), which applies specifically to Miami-Dade and Broward counties, Palm Beach County still enforces stringent large-missile impact testing and wind-load resistance requirements under the FBC's wind-borne debris region standards. All impact doors installed in this area must meet or exceed the 150 mph wind speed threshold applicable to the local wind zone.

What this means practically is that any door installed in a Boca Del Mar home must be tested and certified to resist both wind pressure and high-speed projectile impact — simulating the kind of debris that becomes airborne during a hurricane. Products must carry the appropriate Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) or Florida Product Approval number to qualify for installation under the code. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing only installs products from manufacturers whose door systems carry these certifications, and we never cut corners with non-compliant materials or improper glazing.

All impact door installations in Boca Del Mar require permits pulled through the Palm Beach County Building Division, followed by a formal inspection before the project is considered complete and legally compliant. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles this entire process — permit application, scheduling, and inspection coordination — on behalf of every homeowner we work with. This not only ensures your installation is fully above board from a legal standpoint, but also protects your ability to file insurance claims and sell your home in the future without code-related complications.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Products Built for Palm Beach County Conditions
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is proud to install impact doors from two industry-leading manufacturers: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands have built strong reputations in the South Florida market for producing doors that combine certified hurricane performance with the refined aesthetics that Boca Del Mar homeowners expect. Whether you're upgrading a grand front entry, a rear sliding glass door overlooking your pool, or a set of French doors opening to a courtyard, ESW and CWI offer product lines designed for both function and visual appeal.

ESW's impact door systems are engineered to meet the stringent demands of Florida's wind-borne debris region requirements, featuring heavy-duty aluminum or composite frames, laminated impact-resistant glass, and multi-point locking systems that engage multiple lock points simultaneously along the door frame. This design dramatically increases both security and structural resistance compared to traditional single-point locks. CWI similarly produces doors with reinforced frames and certified impact glazing, with a wide range of styles, finishes, and glass options to complement the architectural character of homes throughout Whisper Walk, Boca Lago, and beyond.

Both manufacturers offer sliding glass door systems that are particularly popular among Boca Del Mar homeowners with open floor plans and outdoor living areas. These systems provide seamless indoor-outdoor flow without compromising on storm protection or energy efficiency. Low-E glass coatings available across both product lines reduce UV penetration and solar heat gain — a meaningful benefit in South Florida's intense sun — while maintaining the clear, bright views that make large glass doors so appealing. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's installation teams are trained in the precise fitting and sealing requirements for each product line, ensuring every door performs exactly as its certification demands.

section_cost.h2: What Boca Del Mar Homeowners Can Expect to Invest in Impact Doors
section_cost.body:
The cost of impact door installation in Boca Del Mar varies depending on the type of door, the size of the opening, the frame material, and the number of units being replaced. A single impact entry door typically ranges from $1,500 to $4,000 installed, while sliding glass door systems and French door configurations can range from $3,000 to $8,000 or more depending on width and glass specifications. For homeowners replacing multiple doors throughout a home — a common scenario given how many Boca Del Mar properties were built before 1994 — bundling installations into a single project often yields better overall pricing and reduces the number of permit cycles required.

When considering these costs against the context of a $470,000 average home value in the 33433 area, the return on investment becomes clear quickly. Impact doors add measurable resale value to your property, improve energy efficiency by reducing air infiltration and solar heat gain, and eliminate the need for hurricane shutters — saving time and physical effort each storm season. They also meaningfully reduce the risk of catastrophic interior damage that can result from a door breach during a storm, a scenario that can cost tens of thousands of dollars in remediation alone.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes this upgrade accessible to more homeowners through flexible financing options, including plans starting as low as $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified applicants. We believe that every Boca Del Mar homeowner deserves the security and peace of mind that properly installed impact doors provide — not just those who can pay in full upfront. Our team will walk you through financing options during your free consultation so you can make the decision that works best for your budget.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for Boca Del Mar Homeowners Who Install Impact Doors
section_insurance.body:
One of the most financially compelling reasons to invest in impact doors in Palm Beach County is the potential for significant homeowners insurance savings. Florida insurance carriers are required to offer premium discounts to policyholders who can demonstrate that their homes meet wind mitigation standards — and properly installed, permitted impact doors are a key qualifying factor. Many Boca Del Mar homeowners who complete a wind mitigation inspection after installing impact products report annual premium savings ranging from several hundred to several thousand dollars, depending on their carrier, coverage level, and the extent of their upgrades.

The wind mitigation inspection process involves a licensed inspector documenting the protective features of your home, including the type and rating of all exterior openings. When your impact doors carry current Florida Product Approval numbers and were installed with proper permits, this documentation is straightforward and works in your favor. Common carriers operating in Palm Beach County — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and several private-market insurers — all factor opening protection into their wind mitigation premium calculations.

Beyond rate reductions, impact doors can also strengthen your position in the claims process should a storm cause other types of damage to your property. Demonstrating that your home's openings were properly protected can help establish that reasonable precautions were taken, potentially smoothing the path for other claims. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides full documentation of all installed products and permit records, which you can submit directly to your insurance agent or inspector. For Boca Del Mar homeowners navigating an increasingly difficult insurance market, every advantage matters — and impact doors are one of the most reliable tools available.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors if Boca Del Mar is six miles from the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Coastal proximity is just one factor in hurricane risk. Boca Del Mar sits in a 150 mph wind zone, and storms like Wilma in 2005 and Irma in 2017 caused serious wind and debris damage throughout inland Palm Beach County communities. Impact doors are required by Florida Building Code for all exterior openings in wind-borne debris regions — regardless of how far you are from the water. The protection they offer is just as critical six miles inland as it is on the shoreline.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors typically cost in Boca Del Mar, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Impact door costs in Boca Del Mar generally range from $1,500 to $4,000 for a single entry door installation and $3,000 to $8,000 or more for sliding glass or French door systems, depending on size and specifications. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified homeowners. During your free consultation, we'll provide a detailed quote and walk you through financing options so you can choose the plan that works best for your budget.

faqs.q: What permits are required for impact door installation in Palm Beach County?
faqs.a: All impact door replacements in Boca Del Mar require a permit from the Palm Beach County Building Division. The permit ensures the installation meets Florida Building Code requirements for the local 150 mph wind zone. Installed products must carry valid Florida Product Approval numbers or Miami-Dade NOA certifications. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit application, scheduling, and inspection coordination process on your behalf — you won't need to navigate any of the paperwork or bureaucracy yourself.

faqs.q: How long does impact door installation take for a typical Boca Del Mar home?
faqs.a: For most single-family homes in Boca Del Mar, impact door installation is completed in one to two days depending on the number and type of doors being replaced. The process includes removing existing doors, preparing the rough opening, installing and sealing the new impact unit, and completing all interior and exterior trim work. Permitting timelines can add a few days to a couple of weeks on the front end, depending on Palm Beach County's current processing volume. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages all scheduling and keeps you informed throughout the process.

faqs.q: Will installing impact doors lower my homeowners insurance premium in Palm Beach County?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes. Florida law requires insurance carriers to offer discounts to homeowners who can document qualifying wind mitigation features, and properly installed impact doors are among the most valuable qualifying factors. Many Boca Del Mar homeowners see meaningful annual premium reductions after completing a wind mitigation inspection following their impact door installation. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides full documentation of installed products and permits to support your wind mitigation report and insurance submission.
